company_profile.industry: Retail multi-asset trading and investing platform
company_profile.geography: eToro holds licences with the FCA in the UK, CySEC in Cyprus, ASIC in Australia, US regulators, Abu Dhabi and several EU states.
company_profile.headquarters: Tel Aviv, Israel (US headquarters: Hoboken, New Jersey)
company_profile.employee_count: GAP — not disclosed in source material
company_profile.description: Yoni Assia, his brother Ronen and David Ring founded the company in Tel Aviv in 2007 as RetailFX. It listed on Nasdaq on 14 May 2025 at $52 a share, raising about $620 million. eToro holds licences with the FCA in the UK, CySEC in Cyprus, ASIC in Australia, US regulators, Abu Dhabi and several EU states. It serves roughly 40 million registered users and 4.23 million funded accounts across about 75 countries. Users trade equities, ETFs, commodities, currencies, CFDs and more than 150 cryptoassets across 25 stock exchanges. Copy trading sits at the centre of the product: users replicate the portfolios of other traders. Q1 2026 was the company's strongest quarter since listing. Evidence note: Financials, KPIs and regulatory facts come from eToro's public filings, FY2025 results, Q1 2026 results and disclosed monthly metrics. Market-sizing figures are analyst-constructed estimates with stated assumptions, not third-party market data. A live engagement would triangulate these against named paid sources. Evidence grade: High. The target is listed on Nasdaq and files quarterly.

key_facts.label: Founders
key_facts.value: Yoni Assia, Ronen Assia, David Ring

key_facts.label: Funded accounts by region
key_facts.value: Europe and UK 72%, APAC 14%, Americas 10%, Middle East and Africa 4%

business_model.revenue_model: eToro reports five streams as "net contribution", which reached $868M in FY2025 and $258M in Q1 2026 alone. Capital markets trading (equities, commodities, currencies) now carries the business. It hit a record $166M in Q1 2026, up 71% year on year. Commodities alone drove about 60% of trading commissions. Crypto produces enormous volume and thin margin. Crypto net trading contribution fell to $13M in Q1 2026, and crypto trades dropped 31% year on year by May. eToro keeps investing here through the Zengo acquisition and its on-chain roadmap, but crypto no longer drives profit. Net interest on customer cash and margin balances contributed $48M in Q1 2026, down 5% year on year as rates eased. Interest-earning assets stood at $7.2B. eToro Money, the neo-banking arm, reached $29M in Q1 2026, up from $22M a year earlier. Money transfers doubled year on year to $1.6B in May. Subscriptions and other covers premium tiers at around $5 a month, the App Store and blockchain rewards.
business_model.value_proposition: A single app where retail investors trade equities, commodities, currencies, crypto and CFDs, and can copy the portfolios of successful traders instead of picking positions themselves. Breadth plus social proof, aimed at users who lack the time or confidence to trade unassisted.
key_activities: Platform engineering across five asset classes.
Running the social and copy-trading network.
AI product development, including the Tori agent built with xAI and Agent Portfolios.
Regulatory licensing and compliance in every market.
key_resources: The copy-trading network and the community of traders that populates it.
A $1.3 billion cash balance funding acquisitions and buybacks.
The brand, and a registered base of about 40 million users.
channels: Direct to consumer through mobile and web.
Community and social features that generate organic acquisition, supported by paid marketing.
The eToro App Store as a partner and ecosystem channel.
business_model.customer_relationships: Self-directed and community-mediated. Users interact with each other as much as with the platform, and copy trading turns other users into the product. Switching costs are low, so the community and the multi-product relationship through eToro Money carry the retention burden.
cost_structure: Cost of crypto revenue dominates the gross cost line and moves with crypto volume.
Customer acquisition and marketing follow, and both are rising: adjusted operating expenses grew 7% quarter on quarter on higher acquisition costs.

market_overview.narrative: eToro competes in retail online trading and investing, a large market whose revenue swings with retail participation, volatility and interest rates. Retail money moved out of crypto and into commodities through the first half of 2026. eToro's asset breadth let it capture that rotation: capital markets trades rose 90% year on year in Q1 to 243 million, and total invested amount climbed to $50 billion from $39 billion. A second shift matters more for margin. Automated and agentic investing is changing how users trade. Copy trading, Agent Portfolios and the App Store generate far more trades of far smaller size. Invested amount per trade fell about 25% to $197. Management confirmed that agentic and copy flows carry a lower take rate than self-directed trading. Engagement is climbing faster than revenue per trade.
market_overview.market_size_summary: No independent third-party market-size figure is cited in the source material. See Market Sizing (TAM/SAM/SOM) for the analyst-constructed estimate, built from eToro's disclosed economics and stated penetration assumptions rather than third-party market data.
market_overview.regional_notes: eToro holds licences with the FCA in the UK, CySEC in Cyprus, ASIC in Australia, US regulators, Abu Dhabi and several EU states, serving roughly 40 million registered users and 4.23 million funded accounts across about 75 countries. Funded accounts by region: Europe and UK 72%, APAC 14%, Americas 10%, Middle East and Africa 4%.

tam.value: Low tens of billions of dollars a year (analyst-constructed estimate)
tam.description: Global retail online trading and investing generates a revenue pool in the low tens of billions of dollars a year, spanning brokerage, CFDs, crypto intermediation and net interest income.

sam.value: Mid-single-digit billions of annual revenue (analyst-constructed estimate)
sam.description: Multi-asset retail trading in the markets where eToro holds licences (UK, EU, Australia, US, GCC, APAC) accounts for an estimated mid-single-digit billions of annual revenue.

som.value: Roughly $1 billion annualised run rate, based on Q1 2026 net contribution of $258M
som.description: eToro captures roughly $1 billion on an annualised run rate, based on Q1 2026 net contribution of $258M. It converts about 11% of its 40 million registered users into 4.23 million funded accounts, which leaves the funnel as the largest untapped lever. Europe and the UK hold 72% of funded accounts, so APAC at 14%, the Americas at 10% and MEA at 4% all sit below their market weight.

tam_sam_som.methodology_note: Analyst-constructed estimate. We built this from eToro's disclosed economics and stated penetration assumptions rather than third-party market data. Treat it as directional. A live engagement would triangulate against named paid sources. Market size does not constrain this business. The question worth diligence is whether the revenue mix holds its take rate as trading shifts toward automated, lower-value transactions.

industry_trends.title: Volatility drives the sector, and nobody controls it
industry_trends.description: The 2026 commodities surge produced eToro's record quarter. Asset breadth protects against rotation between classes. It offers nothing against a broad calm-market regime that suppresses activity everywhere at once.

industry_trends.title: Agentic and automated retail investing
industry_trends.description: Copy trading, AI agents and Agent Portfolios have already generated over 500,000 trades. They raise trade counts and cut invested amount per trade, and they carry a lower take rate. The shift cuts both ways.

industry_trends.title: Rates are normalising
industry_trends.description: Net interest contribution fell 5% year on year in Q1 2026. A tailwind has become a mild headwind.

industry_trends.title: Tokenisation and on-chain infrastructure
industry_trends.description: eToro bought Zengo, a self-custodial wallet provider, in April 2026 and has stated ambitions in perpetuals and prediction markets. The prize is large and the timing is unknown.

industry_trends.title: Regulatory pressure on CFDs and crypto
industry_trends.description: UK and EU regulators keep targeting the highest-leverage retail products.

competitor_landscape.competitive_intensity_narrative: eToro competes on network effects and asset breadth, not price. Commission-free challengers squeeze it from below, and super-apps and crypto natives press from either side. Q1 2026 showed the breadth is real: no single-asset competitor could have absorbed a crypto collapse and still posted a record quarter.

customer_segments.name: Copy and automated investors (the core, and growing)
customer_segments.description: Mainstream retail users who let other traders or AI agents make their decisions. The largest segment by count.
customer_segments.estimated_size: GAP — not disclosed in source material
customer_segments.buying_behavior: Small and frequent trades, with invested amount per trade down to about $197. High engagement. Increasingly served by copy trading and Agent Portfolios, which earn eToro a lower take rate than self-directed trading.

customer_segments.name: Active multi-asset traders (highest value)
customer_segments.description: Self-directed users trading equities, commodities, currencies and CFDs across the platform's full breadth.
customer_segments.estimated_size: GAP — not disclosed in source material
customer_segments.buying_behavior: Higher volume and higher value. This group drove the record $166M capital markets contribution and the commodities surge.

customer_segments.name: Crypto-led users (contribution falling)
customer_segments.description: Users who came for crypto and stayed for it. Once the engagement engine, now a minor profit contributor at $13M in Q1 2026.
customer_segments.estimated_size: GAP — not disclosed in source material
customer_segments.buying_behavior: Deeply cyclical. Crypto trades fell 31% year on year by May 2026. Valuable at the top of the funnel and for engagement, not for margin.

customer_segments.name: eToro Money users (emerging, strategic)
customer_segments.description: Around 1.75 million users holding cards, IBANs and cash balances through the neo-banking arm.
customer_segments.buying_behavior: Transactional and habitual rather than speculative. Money transfers doubled year on year. This is the stickiest and least cyclical relationship eToro has.

strengths: The diversification thesis survived a live test. Crypto contribution collapsed to $13M and the company still posted a record quarter.
The copy-trading network gives eToro a moat competitors cannot buy.
Record Q1 2026: $258M net contribution, $82M net income, $109M adjusted EBITDA.
Licences across roughly 75 countries and $1.3B in cash funding acquisitions.
Early AI positioning through Tori, Agent Portfolios and the App Store, with reported productivity gains.
weaknesses: Growth depends on volatility. A commodities surge produced the record quarter, and management said so.
Take-rate quality is eroding. Invested amount per trade fell 25% to 48%, and copy and agentic flows earn less per trade.
Customer acquisition costs are climbing. Operating expenses rose 7% quarter on quarter, and management attributed funded-account growth to marketing spend.
Net interest contribution is now shrinking, down 5%.
Europe and the UK hold 72% of funded accounts.
opportunities: Converting the funnel: 40 million registered users against 4.23 million funded accounts.
Underweight regions: MEA at 4% of funded accounts, the Americas at 10%, APAC at 14%. The Abu Dhabi licence opens the Gulf.
eToro Money, where transfers doubled year on year and revenue is uncorrelated with trading volatility.
Tokenisation, perpetuals and prediction markets, accelerated by Zengo.
The App Store and subscriptions as recurring revenue.
threats: A calm market. Low volatility suppresses activity in every asset class at once, and breadth is no defence.
Trading 212 and XTB taking European users on price.
Revolut converging from banking, Coinbase from crypto.
Regulators tightening on CFDs and crypto.

forces.rationale: CFD incumbents, commission-free challengers, crypto exchanges and neo-banks all compete for the same retail user.
Licences and the copy-trading network deter newcomers, but large neo-banks and fintechs cross over with user bases already built.
Users switch platforms easily and compare prices. The community and multi-product relationships do the retention work.
eToro depends on liquidity providers, custodians and market-data vendors. Upstream venues set crypto intermediation economics, which explains why crypto margin stays thin.
Robo-investing, direct crypto exchanges, prediction markets and traditional brokers each replace part of what eToro offers.

porters_five_forces.overall_attractiveness: The market is attractive and crowded. eToro defends its position with network effects and breadth rather than cost, and Q1 2026 gave that defence its first real test.

commercial_risks.title: Volatility dependence
commercial_risks.description: Management attributed the record quarter to a commodities volatility surge. Multi-asset breadth protects against rotation between asset classes and does nothing against a calm-market regime that suppresses all of them together. This is now the primary risk in the business.
commercial_risks.mitigation: Grow non-cyclical revenue through eToro Money, subscriptions and interest to build a floor.

commercial_risks.title: Take-rate erosion
commercial_risks.description: Trade counts are exploding while invested amount per trade falls 25% to 48%. Management concedes agentic and copy take rates run below self-directed trading.
commercial_risks.mitigation: Subscription tiers, App Store monetisation, premium products.

commercial_risks.title: Rising customer acquisition cost
commercial_risks.description: Funded-account growth came from marketing spend, and operating expenses rose 7% quarter on quarter.
commercial_risks.mitigation: Convert the 40 million registered users organically and lean on the social flywheel.

commercial_risks.title: Falling interest income
commercial_risks.description: Net interest contribution is down 5% and will keep falling with rates.

commercial_risks.title: Regulatory action on CFDs and crypto
commercial_risks.description: Core products remain regulatory targets in core markets.

commercial_risks.title: Geographic concentration
commercial_risks.description: Europe and the UK hold 72% of funded accounts, exposing eToro to one regulatory and economic bloc.
commercial_risks.mitigation: Expansion into MEA, APAC and the Americas.

commercial_risks.title: Israel operations
commercial_risks.description: eToro lists its core operations and the regional conflict as disclosed risk factors.

growth_opportunities.title: Funnel conversion
growth_opportunities.description: Around 11% of registered users become funded accounts. Raising that number costs less than buying new users and cuts CAC dependence at the same time.

growth_opportunities.title: eToro Money
growth_opportunities.description: Revenue reached $29M in Q1 2026, up from $22M, and transfers doubled year on year. It earns money whether or not markets move, which is the natural hedge against volatility dependence.

growth_opportunities.title: Underweight geographies
growth_opportunities.description: MEA holds 4% of funded accounts and the Americas 10%, against 72% in Europe and the UK. The Abu Dhabi licence gives eToro a Gulf bridgehead.

growth_opportunities.title: AI and the App Store
growth_opportunities.description: Tori, Agent Portfolios and the App Store drive engagement and could carry subscription revenue. They also dilute take rate, so how eToro prices them will decide whether they add or subtract.

growth_opportunities.title: Tokenisation and perpetuals
growth_opportunities.description: Zengo and the on-chain roadmap position eToro for a shift whose timing nobody can call.

growth_opportunities.title: Wealth management and subscriptions
growth_opportunities.description: Recurring, higher-margin revenue that cuts cyclicality.

key_questions.question: How much of Q1 2026 came from the commodities volatility spike, and what does the P&L look like in a quiet quarter across every asset class?

Invested amount per trade fell 25% to 48%, and agentic and copy flows earn less than self-directed trading. What is the net revenue effect once automated flows become the majority, and at what point does engagement growth stop covering the shortfall?
Management attributed funded-account growth to marketing spend. What are blended CAC, payback period, and the organic share of new funded accounts?
40 million registered users, 4.23 million funded. Why has that ratio not improved, and what is the plan and target?
Net interest contribution is down 5%. What happens under another 200 basis points of cuts?
Europe and the UK hold 72% of funded accounts. What is the investment case and timeline for MEA and the Americas?
Crypto contribution is down to $13M. What is the return case for continued investment in Zengo, on-chain and perpetuals against redeploying that capital?
With $1.3B in cash, an active buyback and a live acquisition programme, what is the hurdle rate for deployment?

executive_summary.overview: eToro delivered its strongest quarter since listing. Q1 2026 net contribution reached $258M, up 19%. GAAP net income reached $82M, up 37%. Adjusted EBITDA reached $109M, up 35%. Funded accounts hit 4.23 million by May. The obvious bear case has already been tested and it failed: crypto net trading contribution fell to $13M and crypto trades dropped 31% year on year, yet eToro posted a record quarter anyway as users rotated into commodities, which now drive around 60% of trading commissions, and capital markets contribution surged 71% to a record $166M. The multi-asset flywheel absorbed the shock.
key_findings: Q1 2026 net contribution $258M (+19%), GAAP net income $82M (+37%), adjusted EBITDA $109M (+35%), funded accounts reaching 4.23 million by May 2026

The obvious bear case — crypto dependence — has already been tested and it failed: crypto net trading contribution fell to $13M and crypto trades dropped 31% year on year, yet eToro posted a record quarter as users rotated into commodities (around 60% of trading commissions) and capital markets contribution surged 71% to a record $166M

The risk that remains sits one level deeper: this business depends on volatility itself. Asset breadth protects eToro when money rotates between asset classes, and protects it not at all when markets go quiet and activity falls everywhere at once

Take-rate quality is eroding as copy and agentic trading replace self-directed trading, cutting invested amount per trade by 25% to 48% and earning less on each one, and growth is bought rather than earned, with operating expenses up 7% quarter on quarter on marketing spend
The interest-income tailwind has turned into a headwind, down 5%
investment_thesis_notes: The diversification thesis has been proven: the multi-asset flywheel absorbed a crypto collapse and eToro still posted a record quarter
The remaining risk sits one level deeper than crypto dependence — the business depends on volatility itself, compounded by eroding take-rate quality and marketing-driven customer acquisition cost
executive_summary.overall_rating: Attractive. The diversification thesis has been proven. Growth still depends on volatility.
executive_summary.recommendation: Proceed to deeper diligence on three questions. First, model the through-cycle earnings profile in a low-volatility environment, which is the largest unknown in this business. Second, quantify the net monetisation effect of the shift toward automated trading, where engagement is rising faster than revenue per trade. Third, separate organic from paid funded-account growth and test whether the 40-million-user funnel converts without escalating CAC. The franchise is real, the diversification works, and the balance sheet is strong. What the valuation turns on is whether earnings hold up when markets go quiet.
